member.name: T:Simulog.Effect
member.summary: Base class for effects of IEvent predicates

member.name: T:Simulog.Event`1
member.summary: A 1-argument event (table predicate). When used as intended - with OccursWhen calls - this is an intensional/derived table and as such will have it's contents recomputed every tick.
member.typeparam: Type of the one argument (one column)

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`1.#ctor(System.String,TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`0})
member.summary: Make a new Event predicate with the specified name and default arguments
member.param: Name of the Event
Default variable of the first argument

member.name: P:Simulog.Event`1.Chronicle
member.summary: Access (and create if not created) the event chronicle for this event.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`1.OccursWhen(TED.Interpreter.Goal[])
member.summary: Establishes the logic for when an event occurs.
member.param: Conditions under which the event should occur. When they conditions evaluate to True the values stored in the default variable will be added to the table this tick.
member.returns: The same Event for method chaining.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`1.Causes(Simulog.Effect[])
member.summary: Establishes the logic for side effects of when an event occurs.
member.param: List of Effects that this event occuring will cause.
member.returns: The same Event for method chaining.

member.name: P:Simulog.Event`1.Item(TED.Term{`0})
member.summary: Make a goal from this predicate with the specified argument value.
member.param: Value to check for in the table.

member.name: T:Simulog.Event`1.EventGoal
member.summary: Modified table goal for events - representing the application of arguments to an event.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`1.EventGoal.#ctor(Simulog.Event{`0},TED.Term{`0})
member.summary: Create EventGoal for the given event and argument.
member.param: Event to apply argument to.
Argument to apply to event.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`1.EventGoal.At(TED.Term{Time.TimePoint})
member.summary: Access this events chronicle to find occurrences of the event at the specified time.
member.param: TimePoint to find occurrences of this event at.
member.returns: Goal of finding the occurrences of EventGoal at the time passed in.

member.name: T:Simulog.Event`2
member.summary: A 2-argument event (table predicate). When used as intended - with OccursWhen calls - this is an intensional/derived table and as such will have it's contents recomputed every tick.
member.typeparam: Type of the first argument (column one)
Type of the second argument (column two)

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`2.#ctor(System.String,TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`0},TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`1})
member.param: Name of the Event
Default variable of the first argument
Default variable of the second argument

member.name: P:Simulog.Event`2.Item(TED.Term{`0},TED.Term{`1})
member.summary: Make a goal from this predicate with the specified argument values.
member.param: Value to check for in column one of the table.
Value to check for in column two of the table.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`2.EventGoal.#ctor(Simulog.Event{`0,`1},TED.Term{`0},TED.Term{`1})
member.summary: Create EventGoal for the given event and arguments.
member.param: Event to apply arguments to.
Argument to apply to event (column one).
Argument to apply to event (column two).

member.name: T:Simulog.Event`3
member.summary: A 3-argument event (table predicate). When used as intended - with OccursWhen calls - this is an intensional/derived table and as such will have it's contents recomputed every tick.
member.typeparam: Type of the first argument (column one)
Type of the second argument (column two)
Type of the third argument (column three)

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`3.#ctor(System.String,TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`0},TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`1},TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{`2})
member.param: Name of the Event
Default variable of the first argument
Default variable of the second argument
Default variable of the third argument

member.name: P:Simulog.Event`3.Item(TED.Term{`0},TED.Term{`1},TED.Term{`2})
member.summary: Make a goal from this predicate with the specified argument values.
member.param: Value to check for in column one of the table.
Value to check for in column two of the table.
Value to check for in column three of the table.

member.name: M:Simulog.Event`3.EventGoal.#ctor(Simulog.Event{`0,`1,`2},TED.Term{`0},TED.Term{`1},TED.Term{`2})
member.param: Event to apply arguments to.
Argument to apply to event (column one).
Argument to apply to event (column two).
Argument to apply to event (column three).

member.summary: A 4-argument event (table predicate). When used as intended - with OccursWhen calls - this is an intensional/derived table and as such will have it's contents recomputed every tick.
member.typeparam: Type of the first argument (column one)
Type of the second argument (column two)
Type of the third argument (column three)

member.summary: A 5-argument event (table predicate). When used as intended - with OccursWhen calls - this is an intensional/derived table and as such will have it's contents recomputed every tick.

member.name: T:Simulog.SimuLang
member.summary: Simulog language definitions - macros that can be used as a DSL within C#. For easy access to all of the various predicates that Simulog offers simply use this file when importing.

member.name: T:Simulog.Variables
member.summary: Commonly used TED.Vars for types used in this simulator.
member.remarks: Variables will be lowercase for style/identification purposes.

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.state
member.summary: Boolean TED variable to represent the state of...

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.exists
member.summary: Boolean TED variable to represent when something exists

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.count
member.summary: Integer TED variable to represent the count of something

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.time
member.summary: TimePoint TED variable to represent the current time

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.start
member.summary: TimePoint TED variable to represent a start time

member.name: F:Simulog.Variables.end
member.summary: TimePoint TED variable to represent an end time

member.name: T:Time.Clock
member.summary: Internal clock, keeps time during a simulation by ticking along with Simulator.Update

member.name: F:Time.Clock.ClockTick
member.summary: Simulation clock value (measured in 'ticks')

member.name: F:Time.Clock.LastTickDuration
member.summary: Duration of the previous tick

member.name: F:Time.Clock.clockTicks
member.summary: TED variable to allow for referencing to the underlying clock datatype

member.name: M:Time.Clock.Tick
member.summary: Increments the internal clock by 1

member.name: M:Time.Clock.TimePoint
member.summary: Creates a TimePoint from the current clock tick
member.returns: TimePoint representing the current clock tick

member.name: F:Time.Clock.CurrentTimePoint
member.summary: TED Function to access the current clock tick as a TimePoint

member.name: F:Time.Clock.DecrementByLastTick
member.summary: Decrements the value passed in by the last ticks duration (1)

member.name: F:Time.Clock.CheckTimeWithinLastTick
member.summary: Checks whether or not the TimePoint has occured yet

member.name: T:Time.TimePoint
member.summary: Record of a point in time at the finest possible temporal resolution.

member.name: F:Time.TimePoint.Clock
member.summary: Clock tick value that this time point represents

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.#ctor(System.UInt32)
member.param: Tick value

member.name: F:Time.TimePoint.Eschaton
member.summary: The last possible TimePoint (in this case uint.MaxValue)

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Equality(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if both TimePoints represent the same clock tick
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if both TimePoints represent the same clock tick

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Inequality(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if both TimePoints represent different clock ticks
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if both TimePoints represent different clock ticks

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Equality(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is the same as the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is the same as the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Inequality(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is different from the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is different from the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_GreaterThan(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1 is greater (comes later in time) than t2
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1 is greater (comes later in time) than t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_LessThan(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1 is less (comes sooner in time) than t2
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1 is less (comes sooner in time) than t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_GreaterThan(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is greater than the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is greater than the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_LessThan(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is less than the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is less than the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1 is greater (comes later in time) than or equal to t2
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1 is greater (comes later in time) than or equal to t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_LessThanOrEqual(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1 is less (comes sooner in time) than or equal to t2
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1 is less (comes sooner in time) than or equal to t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_GreaterThanOrEqual(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is greater than or equal to the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is greater than or equal to the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_LessThanOrEqual(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Checks if TimePoint t1s clock tick is less than or equal to the value t2
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: true if TimePoint t1s clock tick is less than or equal to the value t2

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Addition(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Add TimePoints creating a new point representing a clock tick that is the sum of each TimePoints clock ticks
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: a TimePoint representing a clock tick that is the sum of each TimePoints clock ticks

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Subtraction(Time.TimePoint,Time.TimePoint)
member.summary: Subtract TimePoints creating a new point representing a clock tick that is the difference of the TimePoints clock ticks
member.param: TimePoint 1
TimePoint 2
member.returns: a TimePoint representing a clock tick that is the difference of the TimePoints clock ticks

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Addition(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Add some number of clock ticks to a TimePoints clock ticks
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: a TimePoint representing the time t2 number of clock ticks after (assuming positive t2) t1

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.op_Subtraction(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Subtract some number of clock ticks to a TimePoints clock ticks
member.param: TimePoint
number of clock ticks
member.returns: a TimePoint representing the time t2 number of clock ticks before (assuming positive t2) t1

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.AddToTimePointImplementation(Time.TimePoint,System.UInt32)
member.param: number of clock ticks
member.returns: a TimePoint representing the time y number of clock ticks after (assuming positive y) x

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.ToString
member.returns: TimePoint as an integer string with the text "Has not occurred" for Eschaton TimePoints.

member.name: M:Time.TimePoint.FromString(System.String)
member.summary: For use by CsvReader. Takes a string and converts it to a TimePoint.

member.name: T:Utilities.ChronicleIndexing
member.summary: Helper methods for properly handling joint keys and indices.

member.name: M:Utilities.ChronicleIndexing.DemoteKey``1(TED.Interpreter.IColumnSpec{``0})
member.summary: Demotes a key to an index (while maintaining appropriate joins)
member.param: Column to be demoted
member.typeparam: Type of the column
member.returns: Demoted ColumnSpec

member.name: T:Utilities.StringProcessing
member.summary: Handles display functions like quote strings and comma separated lists

member.name: M:Utilities.StringProcessing.QuoteString(System.String)
member.summary: Adds quotation marks to the input string
member.param: string to be quoted
member.returns: input string with quotation marks surrounding it

member.name: M:Utilities.StringProcessing.CommaSeparated``1(System.String,System.Func{System.String,``0})
member.summary: Take string of comma separated list and parse into component parts
member.param: string with comma separated list
function to parse individual items in list
member.typeparam: type of the parsed items
member.returns: array of items of type T
